title: Setting Device Owner doesn't work
author: akama-aka
body:
I only have one account which I can't remove anywhere (which makes sense I can't delete it because its the main & default user). If I click on "Remove/Check Accounts" I'm just getting the account & Autofill Settings Tab opened which has completely nothing in it. As I tried to continue and entered the "adb shell dpm set-device-owner com.bos.oculess/.DevAdminReceiver" command I got this Issue:

```
Exception occurred while executing 'set-device-owner':
java.lang.IllegalStateException: Not allowed to set the device owner because there are already some accounts on the device.
        at com.android.server.devicepolicy.DevicePolicyManagerService.enforceCanSetDeviceOwnerLocked(DevicePolicyManagerService.java:10995)
        at com.android.server.devicepolicy.DevicePolicyManagerService.setDeviceOwner(DevicePolicyManagerService.java:9639)
        at com.android.server.devicepolicy.DevicePolicyManagerServiceShellCommand.runSetDeviceOwner(DevicePolicyManagerServiceShellCommand.java:257)
        at com.android.server.devicepolicy.DevicePolicyManagerServiceShellCommand.onCommand(DevicePolicyManagerServiceShellCommand.java:87)
        at com.android.modules.utils.BasicShellCommandHandler.exec(BasicShellCommandHandler.java:97)
        at android.os.ShellCommand.exec(ShellCommand.java:40)
        at com.android.server.devicepolicy.DevicePolicyManagerService.onShellCommand(DevicePolicyManagerService.java:11433)
        at android.os.Binder.shellCommand(Binder.java:1093)
        at android.os.Binder.onTransact(Binder.java:913)
        at android.app.admin.IDevicePolicyManager$Stub.onTransact(IDevicePolicyManager.java:6329)
        at android.os.Binder.execTransactInternal(Binder.java:1369)
        at android.os.Binder.execTransact(Binder.java:1300)
```

title: [Feature Request] Use new root exploit (Eureka) to do more stuff
author: ilovecats4606
body: With the new exploit with id of `uid=0(root) gid=0(root) groups=0(root) context=u:r:kernel:s0`, and SELinux `Permissive` on Eureka, this could be updated to do more stuff. The exploit is present in v79 at the time of writing.

title: Advice on installing on an old Quest 2 without updating
author: AtlasC0R3
body:
My apologies if this issue is outside the scope of this project, as it is more about asking for advice on using this project in a likely very unusual situation. If this is not the right place to ask, please indicate where else I may find answers.

I have a Quest 2 from an acquaintance that has not been turned on or updated for a while (likely ~2 years), as such, it is probably outdated and likely does not have the developer mode turned on.
How would I go about enabling developer mode on the device, then installing Oculess, all while preventing updates (that will render Oculess useless/incompatible)? Should I manually flash an update up to v41 perhaps for better game compatibility?
Or is it somehow possible to update the device, enable developer mode, then downgrade where Oculess is relevant?

title: I cant uninstall oculess!

body: i am not able to uninstall oculess from my quest to. i read the readme but found nothing. pls help

title: Telemetry isn't stopping
author: Galenmark
body:
Greetings:
I recently downloaded Ocu-less on my Quest 2 and Quest 3, from everything I hear this is a one way trip, the only way to go back is to factory reset. However, a simple restart and everything comes back online the Meta store, friends list, and Horizon Feed. Usually a quick reactivation of Ocu-less fixes this and everything appears to be disabled, but obviously something appears to be amiss. I quickly did a test run on my headset with Ocu-less and my internet activated and jumped into Resident Evil 4. I then quit the game and went to the Meta website under "Your information and permissions" and scrolled down to "App Presence Activity" sadly Meta has a full record of me jumping into Resident Evil 4 at that date and time. Suppose am simply curious if Ocu-less is blocking biometric data, mic data, and camera data. 
So the question is obvious, is Ocu-less operational at this point in time, or am I doing something wrong? Any help would be greatly appreciated.

title: Horizon Feed still Accessible
author: Galenmark
body:
Greetings: 
I was here earlier on having issues with "adb shell dpm set-device-owner com.bos.oculess/.DevAdminReceiver" stating I had multiple accounts, this here was human error on my part, I evidently needed to remove the Meta and Oculus account to gain access to telemetry. Nonetheless I digress, my newest question is, does Oculess work on the most recent Quest 3 update? After getting the application working I seem to have full access to the Horizon Feed and friends list, how is this possible? If remember correctly all that should of been blocked out right?
Any explanation would be greatly appreciated! :)

title: Ability to block updates/installations of Explore, Oculus Assistant Service, and Guidebook
author: DevanWolf
body:
The updates for those bloatware come back in a while from Oculus Core Mobile Services after uninstalling their updates. This is a problem because I want to keep those 3 disabled and not have it enabled by itself again.

The following package names that Oculess requests to block updates:
com.oculus.explore
com.oculus.assistant
com.oculus.guidebook

title: [Feature Request] disable Explore (or at least it automatically showing on startup)
author: AAGaming00
body:
trying to disable it with `pm disable` yields `java.lang.SecurityException: Shell cannot change component state for com.oculus.explore/null to 2`
However, pm uninstall --user 0 works, but then the store page for Explore loads on startup instead.

title: Audio Service occasionally crashes [fixed]
author: threethan
body:
I've noticed that every once and a while I've been getting popups saying that Oculess keeps crashing, and the audio accessibility service gets killed by the system.

I've worked out the source of this - occasionally PackageManager fetches PackageInfo objects but then fails to retrieve their packageName. I'm not sure what causes this, but it would sometimes happen enough for the OS to kill Oculess for throwing too many errors.

Properly fixing this would require the package list to be refreshed constantly which would cause a performance hit, but surrounding calls in try/catch stops the service from being killed and any issues should be basically unnoticeable.

I just pushed this workaround to main, but I'm gonna let Basti build it with their key whenever they have time so people can update in place. I'm just documenting it here as a known issue.
